proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 are associated with longer pfs_months (positive monotone correlation across the full cohort).
Higher values of feature_099 are associated with shorter pfs_months (negative monotone correlation across the full cohort).
Higher values of feature_092 are associated with longer pfs_months (positive monotone correlation across the full cohort).
Higher values of feature_009 are associated with shorter pfs_months (negative monotone correlation across the full cohort).

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = 0.785 between feature_078 and pfs_months (p<1e-300). Very strong positive monotone association — by far the strongest univariate signal in the dataset.
Spearman rho = -0.1201 between feature_099 and pfs_months (p=5.56e-160). Modest negative monotone association.
Spearman rho = 0.1026 between feature_092 and pfs_months (p=4.43e-117). Modest positive monotone association.
Spearman rho = -0.0468 between feature_009 and pfs_months (p=1.29e-25). Weak but highly significant negative monotone association consistent with a tumor-marker-like covariate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_051=0.
Patients with feature_038=1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_038=0.
Patients with feature_013=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_013=0.
Patients with feature_043=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_043=0.
Patients with feature_109=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_109=0.
Patients with feature_067=1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_067=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_051=1 → 3.703 vs feature_051=0 → 5.055; t-test p<1e-300; mean diff = -1.352 months.
Mean pfs_months: feature_038=1 → 5.089 vs feature_038=0 → 4.117; t-test p<1e-300; mean diff = +0.972 months.
Mean diff feature_013(1−0) = -0.327 months; p=8.53e-56.
Mean diff feature_043(1−0) = -0.305 months; p=5.23e-46.
Mean diff feature_109(1−0) = -0.228 months; p=3.69e-06.
Mean diff feature_067(1−0) = +0.221 months; p=2.28e-04.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the ordinal feature_057 (levels 0/1/2) are associated with shorter pfs_months in a dose-response pattern.
The ordinal features feature_025, feature_075, feature_026, feature_071, feature_096, and feature_033 are each associated with pfs_months in a monotone fashion.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = -0.3596 between feature_057 and pfs_months (p<1e-300). Group means decline 5.27 → 4.05 → 2.91 across feature_057=0/1/2 — clear monotone dose-response pattern.

None of feature_025 (p=0.15), feature_075 (p=0.67), feature_026 (p=0.16), feature_071 (p=0.37), feature_096 (p=0.33), or feature_033 (p=0.55) showed significant Spearman correlation with pfs_months. These ordinals appear unrelated to the outcome.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable OLS, feature_051=1 remains independently associated with shorter pfs_months relative to feature_051=0 (signed coefficient negative).
After mutual adjustment, feature_038=1 remains independently associated with longer pfs_months relative to feature_038=0 (signed coefficient positive).

A linear combination of the top univariate predictors (feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_057, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_043, log(1+feature_009), feature_109, feature_067) explains a large fraction of variance in pfs_months (R² > 0.5).
After multivariable adjustment, feature_067 no longer shows an independent association with pfs_months (its univariate signal is confounded).

analyses.result_summary: OLS adjusted coefficient for feature_051 = -1.3617 months/unit (p<1e-300) — direction matches the univariate negative effect.
OLS adjusted coefficient for feature_038 = +0.9422 months (p<1e-300).
OLS multivariable model R² = 0.8609 (≈86% of variance), F-statistic >>>0. Eleven predictors jointly explain the bulk of pfs_months variability.
Adjusted coefficient for feature_067 = +0.0228 (p=0.316); univariate signal is fully explained by other covariates (no independent effect).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_064 (race: white/hispanic/black/asian/other).
Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_018 (insurance: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA across feature_064 levels: F=0.8724, p=0.479. Group means span only 4.27–4.41 months — no detectable disparity in pfs_months by race in unadjusted comparison.
One-way ANOVA across feature_018 levels: F=1.4749, p=0.219. Group means span only 4.25–4.33 months — no detectable disparity in pfs_months by insurance type.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After clinical adjustment (feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_057, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_043, log(1+feature_009), feature_109), race (feature_064) remains independently associated with pfs_months (joint Wald test significant).
After the same clinical adjustment, insurance (feature_018) remains independently associated with pfs_months (joint Wald test significant).

analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald test for the four non-reference race dummies (vs white) in the clinically-adjusted OLS: F=0.5008, p=0.735. Individual race coefficients ranged from +0.007 to +0.025 months and none reached p<0.05. No evidence of racial disparity in pfs_months conditional on clinical features.

Joint Wald test for the three non-reference insurance dummies (vs private) in the clinically-adjusted OLS: F=0.7184, p=0.541. Individual insurance coefficients ranged from −0.028 to +0.002 and none reached p<0.05. No evidence of insurance-related disparity in pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The detrimental effect of feature_051=1 on pfs_months is modified by feature_078 (i.e., a non-zero feature_078 × feature_051 interaction).
The beneficial effect of feature_038=1 on pfs_months is modified by feature_078 (i.e., a non-zero feature_078 × feature_038 interaction).
The detrimental effects of feature_013=1 and feature_043=1 are modified by feature_078.

analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction coefficient feature_078:feature_051 = -0.00669 (p=1.38e-14). Negative sign indicates the harmful effect of feature_051=1 grows by ~0.007 months per unit increase in feature_078.
OLS interaction coefficient feature_078:feature_038 = 0.0002 (p=0.87). Effectively null — the feature_038 benefit does not vary with feature_078.
feature_078:feature_013 interaction p=0.993; feature_078:feature_043 p=0.191. Neither interaction reaches significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The detrimental effect of feature_051=1 is attenuated (less negative) at higher levels of feature_057, i.e., the feature_057 × feature_051 interaction is positive.
The beneficial effect of feature_038=1 is attenuated (smaller positive) at higher levels of feature_057, i.e., the feature_057 × feature_038 interaction is negative.
The feature_057 × feature_043 and feature_057 × feature_109 interactions are non-zero.

analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction coefficient feature_057:feature_051 = +0.0464 (p=2.76e-04). Positive sign confirms the harmful feature_051 effect is partially blunted in patients with higher feature_057.
OLS interaction coefficient feature_057:feature_038 = -0.0602 (p=1.02e-03). Negative sign confirms the feature_038 benefit shrinks at higher feature_057.
feature_057:feature_043 coef = +0.0353 (p=0.028, marginally significant); feature_057:feature_109 coef = +0.034 (p=0.36, ns).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The detrimental effect of feature_051=1 is modified by biomarker feature_109 (non-zero feature_051 × feature_109 interaction).
The detrimental effect of feature_051=1 is modified by biomarker feature_067 (non-zero feature_051 × feature_067 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_051:feature_109 coef = -0.0087 (p=0.83). Null — feature_109 does not modify the feature_051 effect on pfs_months.
feature_051:feature_067 coef = +0.0691 (p=0.17). Null — feature_067 does not modify the feature_051 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The high-prevalence binary covariates feature_106, feature_005, feature_088, feature_122, feature_007, feature_008, and feature_066 each carry an independent (clinically-adjusted) signal on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: After clinical adjustment, none of these binaries shows a meaningful effect: feature_106 coef=0.004 (p=0.6), feature_005 coef=-0.016 (p=0.04, marginal), feature_088 coef=0.005 (p=0.53), feature_122 coef=-0.009 (p=0.24), feature_007 coef=0.007 (p=0.39), feature_008 coef=0.008 (p=0.32), feature_066 coef=0.003 (p=0.71). All adjusted effects are <0.02 months — these features are not predictors of pfs_months once clinical covariates are accounted for.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among feature_057 levels (0/1/2), the adjusted decrement in pfs_months is monotone — level 1 vs 0 is moderately negative, and level 2 vs 0 is more negative still (dose-response).

analyses.result_summary: With C(feature_057) and continuous covariates: level 1 vs 0 coef = -1.1996 months (p<1e-300); level 2 vs 0 coef = -2.3442 months (p<1e-300). Monotone dose-response confirmed and roughly linear (≈ −1.2 months per ordinal step).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between feature_099 and pfs_months is modified by feature_057 (i.e., a non-zero feature_099 × feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction feature_099:feature_057 coef = +0.00157 (p=0.301). Null — the feature_099 effect on pfs_months is uniform across feature_057 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between log(1+feature_009) and pfs_months is modified by feature_057 (non-zero interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction log_feature_009:feature_057 coef = +0.0201 (p=1.28e-03). Significant positive interaction — the negative log(feature_009) effect is partially blunted at higher feature_057 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The harmful effect of feature_051=1 on pfs_months differs across the five levels of feature_064 (i.e., a non-zero race × feature_051 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald test on the four race × feature_051 interaction terms (vs the asian baseline level): F=0.4322, p=0.785. No evidence of effect-modification by race; the feature_051 detriment is uniform across race groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The harmful effect of feature_051=1 on pfs_months differs across the four levels of feature_018 (i.e., a non-zero insurance × feature_051 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald test on the three insurance × feature_051 interaction terms: F=2.1353, p=0.093. Joint test borderline non-significant. Individual contrasts: medicare (vs medicaid baseline) and private show small positive interaction coefficients (~+0.05 months) suggesting the feature_051 detriment is marginally smaller for those insurance categories than for medicaid, but the overall effect is at best very weak.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The harmful effect of feature_051=1 on pfs_months is modified by feature_092 (non-zero feature_092 × feature_051 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction feature_092:feature_051 coef = +0.0064 (p=0.69). Null — feature_092 does not modify the feature_051 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_057=2 subgroup, the feature_051 effect on pfs_months remains negative and the feature_038 effect remains positive (consistent with main-effect direction).

analyses.result_summary: In the feature_057=2 subgroup (n=7437, mean pfs_months ≈ 2.91): feature_051 coef = -1.3106 months (p<1e-300); feature_038 coef = +0.8821 months (p<1e-200). Direction preserved despite floor effect from low PFS in this stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months departs from linearity — specifically, a positive quadratic term in feature_078 captures additional variance beyond the linear term.

analyses.result_summary: Adding feature_078² to the multivariable model: feature_078² coef = +0.000663 (p<1e-100). Significant convex curvature — pfs_months rises with feature_078 and accelerates upward at higher feature_078 values.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each non-white race category in feature_064 (asian, black, hispanic, other) has different mean pfs_months than the white reference group when compared head-to-head.

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise t-tests vs white: asian diff = -0.01 (p=0.82); black diff = -0.042 (p=0.19); hispanic diff = -0.001 (p=0.97); other diff = +0.091 (p=0.22). All non-significant; |diff| ≤ 0.09 months. No race-based gap in pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive feature_092 effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_057 (non-zero feature_092 × feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction feature_092:feature_057 coef = -0.019 (p=0.101). Borderline non-significant; sign suggests the feature_092 benefit may shrink slightly at higher feature_057, but evidence is weak.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The harmful effect of feature_051=1 and the beneficial effect of feature_038=1 on pfs_months interact (non-zero feature_051 × feature_038 interaction); the joint group means deviate from additive expectation.

analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction feature_051:feature_038 coef = +0.027 months (p=0.17). Null — joint cell means (4.87, 5.81, 3.50, 4.51 for the four feature_051×feature_038 combinations) are consistent with additive main effects; no synergistic interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within tertiles of feature_078 (low, mid, high), the harmful effect of feature_051=1 on pfs_months is consistently negative, with the magnitude growing in higher tertiles (consistent with the significant feature_078 × feature_051 interaction in iteration 7).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified OLS: low feature_078 tertile feature_051 coef = -1.2984 (p<1e-300); mid tertile = -1.3929 (p<1e-300); high tertile = -1.3891 (p<1e-300). Direction uniformly negative; magnitude is similar across tertiles in this stratified linear model — the iteration-7 interaction signal corresponds to a small gradient (≈−0.7 months across the feature_078 range).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After clinical adjustment, none of feature_025, feature_075, feature_096, feature_026, feature_071, feature_033 carries an independent effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All six adjusted ordinal coefficients are essentially zero: feature_025 +0.0009 (p=0.85), feature_075 +0.0032 (p=0.5), feature_096 -0.0012 (p=0.8), feature_026 +0.003 (p=0.52), feature_071 -0.0031 (p=0.12), feature_033 +0.0009 (p=0.85). Confirms these ordinals are non-informative for pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The black-vs-white pfs_months gap is non-zero within at least one of the insurance subgroups (private, medicare, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: Black-white mean pfs_months differences: private -0.048 (p=0.34); medicare -0.052 (p=0.3); medicaid -0.0008 (p=0.99); uninsured -0.031 (p=0.84). All differences |diff|<0.06 months and non-significant. No racial gap emerges within any insurance stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A comprehensive OLS combining feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, C(feature_057), feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_043, log(1+feature_009), feature_109, feature_067, plus feature_051×C(feature_057) and feature_092×C(feature_057) interactions, captures both the main-effect structure and the joint feature_051 × feature_057 effect-modification (joint interaction p < 0.01).
In the same model, the joint feature_092 × C(feature_057) interaction is not significant once main effects are adequately captured.

analyses.result_summary: Final model R²=0.861 (adj R²=0.8609). Joint Wald test on the two feature_051 × C(feature_057) interaction terms: F=7.2655, p=0.0007 — significant. Effect-modification of feature_051 by feature_057 stratum is real and non-trivial.
Joint Wald test on the two feature_092 × C(feature_057) interaction terms: F=2.1561, p=0.116. Not significant — feature_092 main effect adequately captures its association with pfs_months.
